response: TEMPERATURE IS A MEASUREMENT OF THE HEAT ENERGY IN A SUBSTANCE. IT IS A PHYSICAL PROPERTY THAT VARY WITH THE STATE OF THE SUBSTANCE. THE TEMPERATURE OF A BODY IS DIRECTLY RELATED TO THE KINETIC ENERGY OF ITS PARTICLES. IN A CLOSED SYSTEM, THE TEMPERATURE IS CONSTANT UNLESS THERE IS A NET TRANSFER OF HEAT. THE TEMPERATURE OF A SOLUTION IS AFFECTED BY THE TYPE AND AMOUNT OF SOLUTE ADDITION. THE TEMPERATURE OF A MATERIAL CAN BE MEASURED USING A THERMOMETER. THE TEMPERATURE OF A BODY IN CONTACT WITH A COLD SURFACE WILL TEND TO DECREASE. THE TEMPERATURE OF A BODY IN CONTACT WITH A HOT SURFACE WILL TEND TO INCREASE. THE TEMPERATURE OF A BODY CAN BE CHANGED BY APPLYING ENERGY TO IT. THE TEMPERATURE OF A BODY CAN BE CHANGED BY REMOVING ENERGY FROM IT.
